author: @reedofwater
text: In regards to Silent Hill, and video game movies in general, it astounds me how there's already a perfectly good plot available that they just have to follow and cash in, and yet, they refuse, go off the rails, put their own spin on it, chew it up and vomit it out, then give us the finished product and say "Hey, here's the thing you love! Watch and enjoy!"

@reedofwater Exactly.
Would you rather they spend screen time where he's constantly figuring out puzzles while we patiently sit there staring at a screen?

I just said "cut out the puzzles and you'll have the film there and ready." it's basically just a detective/mystery movie with some wierd monsters thrown in. The first Silent Hill was like that, and the structure worked just fine, just change the plot.

author: @elricmon2099
text: Mr Inbetween is indeed a masterpiece. Highly recommended

replies.author: @Great_Watt
replies.text: Mr Inbetween is garbage. A show about a professional hitman who routinely gets away with murder and the cops are never investigating him or even questioning him about any of his jobs. He'd be someone they'd know about and keep an eye on. Police are rarely actually so incompetent that someone can moonlight successfully as a hitman. He literally kills some guys with a machine gun close to where he lives one night and there aren't even any police sirens in the distance or anything. Nobody would call the cops after hearing that? In season 1 he discovers that 1 of his crew is actually an undercover cop (proving that the police know about him), he just lets him go, then it's never mentioned ever again. What, did that cop not gather enough evidence of criminal activity? I thought the bracelet/necklace he gave Ray to give to his girlfriend was going to turn into a setup of Ray accepting stolen goods as a way to arrest him, but nothing happens. The entire show exists to show the lead character as a cool guy who routinely beats people twice his size and gets away with murder with zero police interference. It's like a power fantasy the creator had. I have no idea why people like it, it's so stupid and you have to ignore serious logic deficiencies to enjoy it that go way beyond suspension of disbelief.

text:
Your reviews have been solid for me so far. If this new format enables you to get more reviews out there, I'm all for it. I do enjoy your single subject reviews so I hope those won't be sacrificed for these combo episodes.

Additionally, I do appreciate that your full length reviews clock in at 8 minutes or so each, which is about the limits of my attention span for a single movie review. It is what keeps me coming here rather than the other channels with 20-60 minute rants about a movie they don't recommend.

author: @james_150_
text: 0:44  I was blown away by Silent Hill 2 back in 2001. The story, the atmosphere, the visual design and the music all made video games feel like a genuine art form (the remake held up pretty well too). I’m not in a hurry to let a bad film adaptation mess with those memories.  Such a shame.

replies.author: @geraq0
replies.text: The PS2 original was one of the best games I've ever played in my life. Haven't played the remake but I doubt it could recapture the same magic.

replies.author: @balthazor44
replies.text: Same here. For me, it was specifically the scene with Angela on the burning staircase. That scene was such an emotional gut-punch, and it was the moment where, for me, it transcended from a video game to a work of art.

replies.author: @balthazor44
replies.text: ​@geraq0 I think the remake is ok, especially given the low expectations I had going into it. It turned out way better than I'd expected. However, for me, it's biggest failure is the storytelling. Most of the cutscenes just lack the punch they had in the original. Particularly the Maria in the labyrinth scene...well let's just shorten the list and say virtually every Maria scene. The Angela scenes also just fell flat. The Eddie and Laura scenes are fine, and arguably an improvement in some cases, but they really dropped the ball with Maria and Angela. James is the standout in the remake, they did a pretty good job with him. The gameplay itself is just basically RE4 with a SH reskin. I enjoy it well enough, well enough to have just finished my 9th playthrough. But I just skip all the cutscenes now, because they just do nothing for me.

replies.author: @Valkinsenn
replies.text: ​@geraq0I picked up the remake on Steam recently, and despite some gripes, I've actually been enjoying it quite a bit. Granted, I've been using some mods with it — namely cosmetic mods for some things — but, hey, season to taste, I guess.

replies.author: @geraq0
replies.text: ​@balthazor44 thank you for your comments. I'm not particularly fond of the RE4 style of survival horror (I'd much rather play with fixed camera angles or top-down perspective a la Signalis or Conscript), but hearing the character treatment is uneven makes me even less inclined to try the remake. I have nothing against Bloober Team, I liked The Medium, but I think some things don't need to be remade, it would never be the same. The original had such a mixture of talent and vision that it could never be surpassed.

replies.author: @james_150_
replies.text: ​@balthazor44  I always appreciated how the original Konami team treated players as intelligent, and it really showed. I heard they pulled inspiration from everywhere: Dostoyevsky, Francis Bacon, David Lynch etc. It’s a shame you don’t see many talented devs getting that kind of creative freedom anymore.

replies.author: @james_150_
replies.text: ​​@balthazor44  I used to play games with my brother back in the day, and I remember the two of us playing on in total silence for a few minutes after that scene.

author: @justinschnoor9459
text: I just rewatched Silent Hill 2006 last night.  It's still a pretty solid movie.

replies.author: @JadeRunner
replies.text:
Radha Mitchell does a good job as a protagonist you root for. Andrea from The Walking Dead was pretty good as the lady cop, too. The problem is that the story is just not "Silent Hill". As Drinker says, Chris Gans obviously just does not understand what Silent Hill _is._ I always suspected so, and this new adaptation of Silent Hill 2 - the one that is the most easy to adapt into a good movie - just confirms it.

He thinks Silent Hill is just a horror series about a haunted town full of monsters. The psychology, guilt-trauma and themes of redemption go completely over his head.

replies.author: @Anonymous-iw4hx
replies.text: it sure is, not perfect by any means, but as you say, solid

Yes
I enjoyed it. Not perfect. Nothing is.

replies.text: It's great

replies.author: @shockwavecg
replies.text:
​@JadeRunnerWell said. I enjoy the movie, and as a movie it stands on its own, but there was so much lacking when it comes to being a movie about Silent Hill.

I was so disappointed that Sean Bean was wasted as James. If he'd been given the actual role that was in the game, he would have rocked that role and it would have been on the level of Jacob's Ladder.

Also, shout out to the woman who played the main villain. I can never remember her name, but she kills it in every movie and show she's in.

replies.author: @Daniel_C_Griffin
replies.text: It was certainly better than the Resident Evil films, but Return to Silent Hill left me actually angry. My non Silent Hill friends didn't like the film, and I was just seething. It looks okay, Akira's music was great, but holy fuck how do you ruin one of the best stories in gaming so badly?

replies.author: @TheEnderBand
replies.text: It got close but I still felt like it lacked that kind of Lynchian level of surreality that the games had. Something about it just had this strange gestalt that I've never quite been able to fully articulate

replies.text: ​@Mister.Blobbythat's not an excuse for a script that is a hollow copy of the game, without any of the symbolism, nuance and restraint.

replies.author: @Grabthar191
replies.text: I liked it, but I was a little annoyed they didn't have the father as the protagonist. I think I understand why, so they could do some exposition for the audience outside of Silent Hill.

replies.author: @balthazor44
replies.text: ​@nerostark4320 Yeah, I've seen so many people blaming the budget on this one, but no amount of money was going to help this disaster. Only a totally different script would have saved this. After all, Godzilla Minus One was made on half the budget this film had, and it managed to be an outstanding film. Seems like there's an oddly high number of Christophe Glans shills out there. You can't blame him for this, it was the budget! BS, he wrote this trainwreck, of course he gets the blame.

replies.author: @JacobPaul-ix7oc
replies.text:
It's funny you mention delusions and A.I. together...
2 Thessalonians 2:8-12
 8 And then the lawless one (the Antichrist) will be revealed, whom the Lord will consume with the breath of His mouth and destroy with the brightness of His coming. 9 The coming of the lawless one is according to the working of Satan, with all power, signs, and lying wonders, 10 and with all unrighteous deception among those who perish, because they did not receive the love of the truth, that they might be saved. 11 *And for this reason God will send them strong delusion, that they should believe the lie,* 12 that they all may be condemned who did not believe the truth but had pleasure in unrighteousness.

Revelation 13:11-17
11 Then I saw another beast (the Antichrist) coming up out of the earth, and he had two horns like a lamb and spoke like a dragon (Satan). 
12 And he exercises all the authority of the first beast in his presence, and causes the earth and those who dwell in it to worship the first beast, whose deadly wound was healed (He is the Dictator of an empire that seemed to have lost its status as a world superpower but then regains it) . 
13 He performs great signs, so that he even makes fire come down from heaven on the earth in the sight of men. 
14 And he deceives those who dwell on the earth by those signs which he was granted to do in the sight of the beast, telling those who dwell on the earth to *make an image to the beast* who was wounded by the sword and lived.  (An artificial human, most likely made to resemble Karl Marx, Lenin or Stalin.)
15 *He was granted power to give breath to the image of the beast* (the strong delusion, an artificial human seemingly being alive), that *the image of the beast should both speak and cause as many as would not worship the image of the beast to be killed.* 
16 He causes all, both small and great, rich and poor, free and slave, to receive a mark on their right hand or on their foreheads, 
17 and that no one may buy or sell except one who has the mark or the name of the beast, or the number of his name.

Revelation 14:9-11
9 Then a third angel followed them, saying with a loud voice, “If anyone worships the beast and his image, and receives his mark on his forehead or on his hand, 10 he himself shall also drink of the wine of the wrath of God, which is poured out full strength into the cup of His indignation. He shall be tormented with fire and brimstone in the presence of the holy angels and in the presence of the Lamb. 11 And the smoke of their torment ascends forever and ever; and they have no rest day or night, who worship the beast and his image, and whoever receives the mark of his name.”
  The people who take the mark (Quantum Dot Tattoo Universal ID) will submit to the Antichrist who gives them "liberal freedom" to live as immorally as they please, deceived into believing an artificial human has a soul and that they too can have their souls transferred into a new body.

author: @Parzival.UltimateGamer
text: Silent Hill would only work as a series. You can't marinate in the atmosphere with just a 2 hour movie. It always turns into a haunted house run through of all the monsters.

replies.author: @folding_chable
replies.text:
Problem is it would need to be like a 6-8 episode single season run. And that just ain't happening nowadays imo. With an IP like Silent Hill only one of two things is ever likely to happen; you can either have a 2 hour movie, or you can have a 3-4 season TV/streaming series with 8-10 episodes a pop. 

Nobody will ever do it right, because it's only about $$$.
